subject: [PATCH v2] mm/percpu, memcontrol: Per-memcg-lruvec percpu accounting

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in exporting mod_memcg_lruvec_state, which was unnecessary for this patch and only leftover from another series. The author apologized for the 'noise' and promised no further changes are needed.
reviews.raw_body:
Hello Matthew, thank you for reviewing my patch!

Whoops, no module uses this symbol in this patch. I was working on a similar
patch series (accounting zswap statistics on a per-lruvec level [1]) where I did
the same thing, and needed to export this so that using it in zsmalloc
wouldn't break if zram was built as a module.

Obviously there's no need for exporting for this patch but I forgot to drop
that part when copying this over from that series. Sorry for the noise!

Thanks again for catching this. I hope you have a great day!
Joshua

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/all/20260311195153.4013476-10-joshua.hahnjy@gmail.com/

2026-04-03.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory accounting system to provide more detailed information about per-memcg-lruvec (per-cgroup, per-node, and per-LRU vector) percpu allocations. It does this by converting a certain type of memory tracking from a per-memcg level to a per-node level, allowing for better visibility into memory pressure on multi-NUMA systems. The patch also introduces separate accounting for node-level statistics and memcg-lruvec statistics, which requires exposing a new function to be used outside of the memory control module. This change improves the kernel's ability to track memory usage at a finer granularity, particularly for percpu allocations, but has a small memory overhead and negligible performance impact.

2026-04-04.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory accounting system to provide more detailed information about per-memcg-lruvec (per-cgroup, per-node, and per-LRU vector) percpu allocations. It does this by converting a certain type of memory tracking from a per-memcg level to a per-node level, allowing for better visibility into memory pressure on multi-NUMA systems. The patch also introduces separate accounting for node-level statistics and memcg-lruvec statistics, which requires exposing a new function to be used outside of the memory control module. This change improves the kernel's ability to track memory usage at a finer granularity, particularly for percpu allocations, but has a small memory overhead and negligible performance impact.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch may introduce some inaccuracy by charging based on possible CPUs, but considers it acceptable and plans to address this in v3.
reviews.raw_body:
Hey Harry, thanks for giving this patch a look!

Yes, definitely. I think the solution is just to charge based on possible
CPUs, even if that might lead to some inaccuracy (by however many CPUs
aren't online at that moment). Seems like that's what already happens
in memcg anyways, so I think this discrepancy is OK to tolerate.

Will spin up a v3! Thanks a lot, Harry! Have a great day : -)
Joshua

2026-04-07.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory accounting system to provide more detailed information about per-memcg-lruvec (per-cgroup, per-node, and per-LRU vector) percpu allocations. It does this by converting a certain type of memory tracking from a per-memcg level to a per-node level, allowing for better visibility into memory pressure on multi-NUMA systems. The patch also introduces separate accounting for node-level statistics and memcg-lruvec statistics, which requires exposing a new function to be used outside of the memory control module. This change improves the kernel's ability to track memory usage at a finer granularity, particularly for percpu allocations, but has a small memory overhead and negligible performance impact.

reviews.author: Harry Yoo (Oracle)
reviews.summary: Raised accuracy concerns about charging based on possible CPUs, suggesting a potential discrepancy with existing memcg behavior. Asked for an equivalent of nr_cpus_node() for possible CPUs.

reviews.author: Joshua Hahn
reviews.summary: Responded positively to Harry Yoo's review, agreeing with his concerns and planning to address them in an updated version (v3).

2026-04-08.patch_summary: Joshua Hahn's patch aims to introduce per-memcg-lruvec percpu accounting, but Harry Yoo raises concerns about the accuracy of charging based on possible CPUs.
